raw_text:
EXECUTIVE SUMMARY
November actuals came in at $627,900 against a budget of $550,000, a $77,900 (+14.2%) overage driven predominantly by salary and contractor lines. The salary overage across all five departments — totalling $49,400 — reflects a mix of an early new hire start in Engineering, a transitional double-salary overlap in Product, a mis-coded bonus payment in Marketing, temporary peak-season agency staff in Operations, and a contractor engagement coded to G&A Salaries per CFO instruction. Contractor Spend exceeded budget by $18,500 (+92.5%), fully attributable to a pre-approved platform migration project that is on track and within its $50,000 total project budget.

LINE COMMENTARY

Engineering Salaries | +$18,000 (+15.0%) | Budget: $120,000 | Actual: $138,000
The $18,000 overage is attributed to Staff Engineer Priya Sharma (L5) joining on 28 October rather than the originally planned 4 November start date, resulting in her first full payroll run hitting November earlier than budgeted. An HR payroll variance memo confirms this as the sole driver and indicates December is expected to return to plan; however, exact reconciliation of the $18K figure against her $185K annualised base should be confirmed against the November payroll register.
Sources
- Slack - 2024-11-20T12:15:00Z - slack-engineering_salaries-broad - hr-slack-2024-11-01: Priya Sharma (Staff Engineer, L5) started 28 October — first full salary run hits November. Comp: $185K base. Previous plan assumed November start but she onboarded a week early, creating one extra pay period overlap.
- Gmail - 2024-11-20T18:15:00Z - gmail-engineering_salaries-broad - gmail-2024-11-01-priya-offer: Offer letter to Priya Sharma — start date 28 October per her request. Original plan was 4 November. First full payroll: November.
- Gmail - 2024-11-20T18:45:00Z - gmail-engineering_salaries-narrow - gmail-2024-11-29-payroll-variance: November payroll variance memo from HR. Engineering: +$18K from early start (Priya). Full December return to plan.

Product Salaries | +$12,000 (+15.0%) | Budget: $80,000 | Actual: $92,000
The $12,000 overage is attributed to a double-salary month in which outgoing PM Marcus served his full notice period through 30 November while incoming PM Sarah started on 1 November, resulting in both individuals on payroll simultaneously. This is expected to be a one-month event only, with Marcus having exited at month-end; however, this evidence is single-source (Slack only) — Gmail corroboration was not found across two search passes and formal approval should be confirmed.
Sources
- Slack - 2024-11-16T12:15:00Z - slack-product_salaries-broad - hr-slack-2024-11-04: Note on Product: Marcus (outgoing PM) is serving his notice through 30 November. Sarah (incoming PM) started 1 November. Double salary in November only — Marcus exits end of month.

Marketing Salaries | +$8,400 (+14.0%) | Budget: $60,000 | Actual: $68,400
The $8,400 overage represents Q3 performance bonuses totalling $8,400 paid on 15 November and posted to the Marketing Salaries line, where they appear to have been mis-coded — Slack notes these should have been recorded under a separate bonuses line item. This evidence is single-source (Slack only) and Gmail corroboration of the bonus approval was not found; the posting classification and approval should be confirmed before any reclassification is actioned.
Sources
- Slack - 2024-11-18T12:15:00Z - slack-marketing_salaries-broad - hr-slack-2024-11-15: Q4 performance bonuses paid 15 November for Marketing team. $8,400 total across the team per the approved Q3 performance review outcomes. Separate from base salary line — should have been budgeted under bonuses but posted to salaries.

Operations Salaries | +$7,000 (+17.5%) | Budget: $40,000 | Actual: $47,000
The $7,000 overage reflects five temporary warehouse staff engaged via agency for the November–December peak season, with costs posted to Operations Salaries. Slack notes the requirement was planned in an operations budget review but was not reflected in the original headcount budget; this evidence is single-source (Slack only) and Gmail corroboration was not found, so the budget review documentation and agency approval should be confirmed.
Sources
- Slack - 2024-11-19T12:15:00Z - slack-operations_salaries-broad - ops-slack-2024-11-01: Peak season starts — 5 temporary warehouse staff added for November and December. Bringing in via agency, posting to Ops Salaries. Cost: $7,000 additional per month. Planned in operations budget review, not reflected in original headcount plan.

G&A Salaries | +$4,000 (+13.3%) | Budget: $30,000 | Actual: $34,000
The $4,000 overage relates to finance contractor Clara Mendez engaged at $4,000/month for Q4 year-end close support, posted to G&A Salaries per CFO instruction; the engagement is noted as a three-month arrangement. This evidence is single-source (Slack only) — Gmail corroboration of the CFO approval was not found across two search passes, and formal sign-off documentation should be confirmed. Note that a contractor cost posted to a salaries line may also warrant a coding review.
Sources
- Slack - 2024-11-12T12:15:00Z - slack-g&a_salaries-broad - finance-slack-2024-11-01: Brought in Clara Mendez (finance contractor) for Q4 close support. $4,000/month, posting to G&A Salaries per CFO instruction. Year-end close support, 3-month engagement.

Contractor Spend | +$18,500 (+92.5%) | Budget: $20,000 | Actual: $38,500
The $18,500 overage reflects November spend on two senior contractors (DevOps at $750/day and Backend at $700/day) engaged from 28 October for an eight-week platform migration, with the engagement co-signed by the CTO and CFO under a formal SOW. CRM records confirm this as project PLAT-001, with $18,500 representing spend within a $50,000 total approved project budget at Milestone 2 of 4, currently currently tracking to a January go-live; the monthly budget line was not sized to absorb this project, though total project spend remains within approval.
Sources
- Slack - 2024-11-16T12:15:00Z - slack-contractor_spend-broad - eng-slack-2024-10-28: Platform migration kicked off — two senior contractors (DevOps + Backend) engaged for 8 weeks from 28 Oct. Day rates $750 and $700 per day respectively. Estimated Nov cost ~$12,000. CTO approved.
- Gmail - 2024-11-16T18:15:00Z - gmail-contractor_spend-broad - gmail-2024-10-25-contractor-sow: SOW executed with TechForce Ltd (DevOps contractor) and NovaBuild Inc (Backend contractor). 8-week engagement from 28 Oct. Day rate schedule attached. CTO and CFO co-signed.
- CRM - 2024-11-16T26:15:00Z - crm-contractor_spend-broad - crm-project-PLAT-001: Platform Migration Project. Status: In Progress. Budget: $50,000 (total). Contractor spend Nov: $18,500 of $50K approved budget. Milestone 2 of 4 complete. On track for January go-live.

INSIGNIFICANT VARIANCES
The following lines are below the significance threshold and have not been investigated:
- Sales Salaries: +$6,000 (+4.0%)
- Benefits & Payroll Taxes: +$4,000 (+8.0%)
